claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 7 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child (or children) you want to claim Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know the age(s) of the child(ren) you're asking about. If you have multiple children, please tell me about each of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes, I'm asking about my child Alex. He's 7 years old, so under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent, thank you! Since Alex is under 16, he automatically qualifies based on age. Let me continue with the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in any paid work at all)?

Since Alex is 7 years old, I expect he's not working - but I just need to confirm this to proceed with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work at all - he's only 7!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex **not** receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?

Again, at 7 years old this would be very unusual, but I need to confirm - does Alex receive any benefits in his own name?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you for confirming. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex currently doing an apprenticeship?

At 7 years old, I'm confident the answer is no - but just to confirm, Alex is not enrolled in any kind of apprenticeship programme, correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, of course not - he's only 7, he's in primary school!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely, just ticking the boxes! Let me move on.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now let's check your residency status.**

To be eligible for Child Benefit, you need to meet UK residency requirements. You can qualify through **any one** of the following:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You're a resident living in the UK
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status** - With additional conditions (e.g., working, seeking work, studying, or as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national)
4. **Crown servant abroad** - You live abroad as a Crown servant

Do any of these apply to you? Most commonly, people simply normally live in the UK. Is that the case for you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you! Living in the UK means you meet the residency requirements. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Are you responsible for Alex? You can meet this requirement through **any one** of these ways:

1. **Living with the child** - Alex lives with you
2. **Contributing to their upkeep** - You pay at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards their care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, etc.)
3. **Paying maintenance** - You make maintenance payments under a court order or agreement

Does Alex live with you, or do you contribute to his upkeep in another way?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me, but I do help with his upkeep - I contribute towards things like clothes and his needs.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. As long as you contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount towards Alex's care (through things like clothes, food, pocket money, etc.), you can meet the responsibility requirement. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Important question:**

Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to ask: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

For example, does Alex's other parent or another carer already receive Child Benefit for him?

- **No one else is claiming** Child Benefit for Alex
- **Someone else is claiming**, but they've agreed to transfer the claim to you
- **There's a dispute** about who should receive Child Benefit for Alex

Which of these applies to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that's straightforward. Let's continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Where does Alex currently live? I need to check if any special circumstances apply:

- **In hospital** - Alex is staying in hospital
- **In local authority care** - Alex is in care provided by a local authority
- **Fostered** - Alex is being fostered
- **Normal living arrangement** - Alex lives at home (with his other parent, family member, etc.) in a regular household

Which of these best describes Alex's situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is in local authority care.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for letting me know. There are some specific rules around children in local authority care. Let me check the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Important question about Alex's care situation:**

Since Alex is in local authority care, Child Benefit eligibility depends on the duration and your ongoing contact with him.

You **can still be eligible** if **either** of these applies:

1. **Alex has been in care for 8 weeks or less**, OR
2. **Alex spends at least 24 hours a week at home with you**

Additionally, you may be able to claim again if Alex:
- Spends at least 2 consecutive nights per week with you, OR
- Spends 7 or more days in a row at home with you

Does either of the following apply to your situation?
- Alex has been in care for **8 weeks or less**, OR
- Alex spends **at least 24 hours per week** at home with you

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's been in care for a bit longer than that - a couple of months now - and he doesn't come home to stay with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for being honest about your situation. I need to ask one more question to complete the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, I've reached a decision about your eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Assessment Result: **Not Eligible**

I'm sorry to tell you that you are **not currently eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

### Why?

While you met most of the requirements:
- ✅ Alex is under 16
- ✅ You live in the UK
- ✅ You contribute to Alex's upkeep
- ✅ No one else is claiming for him

**However**, because Alex has been in local authority care for **more than 8 weeks** and does not spend at least **24 hours per week** at home with you, Child Benefit cannot continue.

---

### There is hope for the future

You **can claim Child Benefit again** if Alex's situation changes and he starts:
- Spending **at least 2 consecutive nights each week** with you, OR
- Spending **7 or more days in a row** at home with you

---

### What to do next

- If Alex's care situation changes or he starts spending more time at home, you may be able to make a new claim
- You can contact **HMRC** directly to discuss your specific circumstances — they may be able to provide guidance on your individual case
- Visit [gov.uk/child-benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit) for more information

I understand this isn't the answer you were hoping for. If Alex's situation changes, please don't hesitate to check your eligibility again.

reasonings: Alex is 7 years old (under 16), which would normally qualify him for Child Benefit. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and meets residency requirements. The claimant contributes to Alex's upkeep, which satisfies the responsibility requirement. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. However, Alex is in local authority care and has been there for more than 8 weeks. He also does not spend at least 24 hours a week at home with the claimant. Under the rules, Child Benefit stops after 8 weeks when a child is in care, unless the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home. Therefore, the claimant is currently not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets most eligibility criteria for Child Benefit (UK residency, responsibility for the child, no other claimants). However, because Alex has been in local authority care for more than 8 weeks and does not spend at least 24 hours per week at home, Child Benefit eligibility is not met at this time.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Has the child been in care for 8 weeks or less, OR do they spend at least 24 hours a week at home?
response.help_text: Child Benefit continues for up to 8 weeks in care. Exception if child spends 24+ hours/week at home. Can claim again if child spends 2 consecutive nights/week or 7+ days in a row at home.

age_limits.birthday_rule: Benefit stops on child's 20th birthday, not end of academic year

training_exclusions: Training with employer agreement for future job

payment_rates.note: Current rates not specified in source documents - check gov.uk for latest rates

high_income_charge.note: High Income Child Benefit Charge applies if you or partner earns over £60,000. This is a payment adjustment, not an eligibility criterion.

response.reason: Child has been in local authority care for more than 8 weeks and does not spend at least 24 hours a week at home
response.guidance: Child Benefit stops after 8 weeks in care, unless child spends at least 24 hours a week at home. You can claim again if child spends 2 consecutive nights each week or 7+ days in a row at home. Contact HMRC about your specific situation.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 7 years old (under 16), which would normally qualify him for Child Benefit. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and meets residency requirements. The claimant contributes to Alex's upkeep, which satisfies the responsibility requirement. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. However, Alex is in local authority care and has been there for more than 8 weeks. He also does not spend at least 24 hours a week at home with the claimant. Under the rules, Child Benefit stops after 8 weeks when a child is in care, unless the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home. Therefore, the claimant is currently not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets most eligibility criteria for Child Benefit (UK residency, responsibility for the child, no other claimants). However, because Alex has been in local authority care for more than 8 weeks and does not spend at least 24 hours per week at home, Child Benefit eligibility is not met at this time.
